title: System, method and apparatus for managing resources for virtual machines and device thereof
abstract: A method, system, apparatus and device for managing resources of virtual machines are disclosed by the present disclosure. The method includes obtaining monitoring data of a virtual machine at a client. The method also includes matching the monitoring data with a pre-determined match rule to generate a match result, wherein the match rule determines whether the monitoring data satisfies a match condition of the match rule. The method further includes executing an action for the virtual machine based upon a processing strategy corresponding to the match result.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of managing resources for virtual machines, the method comprising: obtaining monitoring data of a virtual machine at a client; matching the monitoring data with a pre-determined match rule to generate a match result, wherein the match rule determines whether the monitoring data satisfy a match condition of the match rule; and executing an action for the virtual machine based upon a processing strategy corresponding to the match result. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the monitoring data comprises at least one of: a virtual machine type; CPU utilization; memory utilization; disk space utilization; IOPS (Input Output Per Second); bandwidth; and a number of concurrent connections. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the match condition corresponds to: the monitoring data; an operation; and a corresponding operating period for the virtual machine upon a successful match. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the match rule comprises: a rule title; a product category; a virtual machine type; a match condition; an operation; and an operating period. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the match condition comprises at least one of: a CPU utilization threshold; a memory utilization threshold; a disk space utilization threshold; an IOPS threshold; a disk throughput threshold; a slow request number threshold; and a bandwidth utilization threshold. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the match condition further comprises an upper limit of a number of times a threshold of the match condition is reached. 7. The method of claim 4, wherein the operation is selected from the group consisting of: shutting down the virtual machine; sending an alert; and the combination thereof. 8. The method of claim 4, further comprising responsive to a successful match of the monitoring data and the match condition, configuring the operating period to process the virtual machine according to the operation every time or only once. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the matching the monitoring data with a pre-determined match rule to generate a match result comprises: querying a pre-configured rule database regarding a match rule corresponding to a virtual machine type based upon a virtual machine type of the monitoring data; matching the monitoring data with a match condition of the query match rule to determine whether a value of the monitoring data exceeds a threshold of the match condition of the query match rule; and in response to a determination that the value of the monitoring data exceeds the threshold, generating a match result for the virtual machine based on a pre-determined operation of the query match rule, wherein the match result includes the operation for the virtual machine. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising and prior to the generating a match result: determining whether an upper limit is met, wherein the upper limit is a number of times the monitoring data reaches the threshold of the match condition within a pre-determined time interval; and in response to a determination that the upper limit is met, executing a match result for the virtual machine generated based upon the pre-determined operation of the match rule. 11. The method of claim 10, further comprising and prior to the generating a match result: determining whether the virtual machine is processed during a pre-determined time interval; in response to a determination that the virtual machine is not processed, executing the match result for the virtual machine generated based upon the pre-determined operation of the match rule; in response to a determination that the virtual machine is processed, determining whether a pre-determined operating period of the match rule is configured to execute the operations for the virtual machine every time upon a successful match of the monitoring data and the match condition; and 

 in response to a determination that the pre-determined operating period is configured to execute the operations for the virtual machine every time, executing the match result for the virtual machine generated based upon the pre-determined operation of the match rule. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein the executing an action for the virtual machine based upon a processing strategy corresponding to the match result comprises: obtaining an operation for the virtual machine included in the match result; querying a penalty action database regarding an action corresponding to the operation; and executing the action for the virtual machine. 13. The method of claim 1, after the executing an action for the virtual machine based upon a processing strategy corresponding to the match result, further comprising: storing in a monitoring log database the monitoring data and the action executed for the virtual machine. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising and after the storing in a monitoring log database the monitoring data and the action executed for the virtual machine: obtaining a number of actions executed for the virtual machine by use of time information corresponding to when the monitoring data and the action executed for the virtual machine are stored in the monitoring log database; if the number of actions executed for the virtual machine is less than a pre-determined threshold, extending a pre-determined time interval at which the monitoring data is periodically obtained; and if the number of actions executed for the virtual machine is greater than the pre-determined threshold, shortening a pre-determined time interval at which the monitoring data is periodically obtained. 15. An apparatus for managing resources for virtual machines, the apparatus comprising: a processor; and a non-transitory computer-readable medium operably coupled to the processor, the non- transitory computer-readable medium having computer-readable instructions stored thereon to be executed when accessed by the processor, the instructions comprising: 

 a monitoring data obtaining module configured to obtain monitoring data of a virtual machine at a client; a matching module configured to match the monitoring data with a pre-determined match rule to generate a match result, wherein the match rule determines whether the monitoring data satisfies a match condition of the match rule; and a processing module configured to execute an action for the virtual machine based upon a processing strategy corresponding to the match result. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the monitoring data obtained by the monitoring data obtaining module comprises at least one of: a virtual machine type; CPU utilization; memory utilization; disk space utilization; IOPS (Input Output Per Second); bandwidth; and a number of concurrent connections. 17. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the matching module comprises: a rule database query sub-module configured to query a pre-configured rule database regarding a match rule corresponding to the virtual machine type based upon a virtual machine type of the monitoring data; a threshold determination sub-module configured to match the monitoring data with a match condition of the query match rule to determine whether a value of the monitoring data exceeds a threshold of the match condition of the match rule; and a match result generating sub-module configured to receive a determination result from the threshold determination sub-module, and in response to a determination that the value of the monitoring data exceeds the threshold, to generate a match result for the virtual machine based upon a pre-determined operation of the match rule, the match result including the operation for the virtual machine. 18. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the processing module comprises: an operation obtaining sub-module configured to obtain an operation for the virtual machine included in the match result; an action inquiring sub-module configured to query a penalty action database regarding an action corresponding to the obtained operation; and an action executing sub-module configured to execute the action for the virtual machine. 19. An apparatus for transmitting monitoring data to a server, the apparatus comprising: a processor; and a non-transitory computer-readable medium operably coupled to the processor, the non- transitory computer-readable medium having computer-readable instructions stored thereon to be executed when accessed by the processor, the instructions comprising: a monitoring data obtaining module configured to obtain monitoring data of a virtual machine; and a monitoring data transmitting module configured to transmit to the server the obtained monitoring data of the virtual machine. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, wherein the monitoring data comprises at least one of: a virtual machine type; CPU utilization; memory utilization; disk space utilization; IOPS (Input Output Per Second); bandwidth; and number of concurrent connections.
